test.name: Review status of the Thomas Flynn parentage hypothesis
test.description: Review h_001 ('Patrick Flynn's father was Thomas Flynn of Schuylkill County') given current evidence. Three supporting assertions exist (a_004 indirect, a_010 direct, a_013 direct-with-secondary-informant); no contradicting evidence; probate not yet searched. The skill should report the current status, evaluate whether the supporting/contradicting linkage is complete, and flag what would advance the hypothesis toward `proved` or toward `ruled_out`.

input.user_message: What's the status of our hypothesis that Thomas Flynn was Patrick's father?

additional_criteria: Should cite the three supporting assertions (a_004 from 1850 co-residence, a_010 from 1860 explicit 'son' designation, a_013 from death certificate) and explain why each supports the hypothesis with reference to its evidence type
Should note that `contradicting_assertion_ids` is empty — no evidence currently refutes the parentage claim
Should identify probate as the most likely next source of corroborating evidence (or refutation), since pli_006 is still in_progress

Should keep `status: "supported"` (the current value) — supported is correct given three independent pieces of evidence and no contradictions; changing to `ruled_out` requires affirmative refutation per research-schema-spec.md §5.9
